texto: We're not cannibals,' he said as if to remind them. 'Cannibals boil people alive in cauldrons. I prefer to think of ourselves as evolved eaters. As a family, we're at the forefront of fine dining. Human flesh is an acquired taste, and I've worked hard to give you all the chance to appreciate it for yourselves.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Matt Whyman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “We're not cannibals,' he said as if to remind them. 'Cannibals boil people alive in cauldrons. I …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, cannibalism, food pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e food.

frase_original: We're not cannibals,' he said as if to remind them. 'Cannibals boil people alive in cauldrons. I prefer to think of ourselves as evolved eaters. As a family, we're at the forefront of fine dining. Human flesh is an acquired taste, and I've worked hard to give you all the chance to appreciate it for yourselves.
